# docs

Parent: [Gnosis Polyglot](../README.md)

## Purpose

Developer documentation for the Betti -> WASM compute fabric that lives in the
`polyglot` crate. The fabric ahead-of-time compiles source functions in a set
of certified program classes into small, import-free WebAssembly artifacts
carrying a `betti-wasm/1` manifest, with fail-closed admission at compile time
and trap-on-divergence semantics at run time. These four guides cover the full
author-to-caller path: writing and compiling an artifact, the exact contract
formats, scheduling across the deployed Betti worker fleet, and publishing and
economics.

## Key Files

| File | Role |
| --- | --- |
| `BETTI_WASM_GUIDE.md` | Front-door tutorial: write a program in a certified class, compile it with the monster CLI, inspect the manifest, run it locally, on the warm Node harness, the edge fleet, or through swerve. Describes the three emission tiers (A: direct wasm from `src/aot_wasm.rs`; B: freestanding C parity lane; C: gg-walker interpreter-embed). |
| `BETTI_WASM_REFERENCE.md` | Precise contract reference: certified class grammars, CLI flags, the `betti-wasm/1` manifest, `scalar-v1` / `json-v1` ABIs, gg-walker envelope (v1/v2/v3) and cursor formats, the BSNP session-frame spec, and per-lane decline/divergence semantics. |
| `BETTI_MESH_GUIDE.md` | Scheduling certified GG topologies (the foil-fanout class) across the deployed `betti-mesh` coordinator and `betti-station-*` workers: the `POST /schedule` contract, arm evidence, budget-bounded arms with cursor resume, width governance, race mode, and token burn. |
| `BETTI_PUBLISH_GUIDE.md` | Artifact publishing and economics for authors: `gnode wasm-store` with a DID and price, cache-replay earn (70/30 split), caller metering, and the admitted-cache-key mint. |

## Related

Source anchors are in the parent crate (`src/aot_wasm.rs`,
`src/aot_wasm_c.rs`, `src/aot_gg_embed.rs`, `gg-walker-wasm/`) and in
`open-source/gnosis/src/` (TS manifest validator and execution bridge).
Operator-side manuals (fleet operations, economics operations, cache-mint
design) live in `open-source/x-gnosis/docs/`.
